lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Good day! What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Junior Jim told me that you might be able to help me out with sea charting.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Ah, that I can do indeed. You see, I am a map maker extraordinaire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I find that a little hard to believe...

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Don't let this fool you. It takes more than eyes to make a map. They named the mapping book after me, you see...

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Both of them...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: So, as you can now see, I am very much the man to speak to about this sort of thing.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Here, I have a spare spyglass you can have.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: You are quite welcome to use it to admire me from afar!

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: So, what specifically would you like to know about charting?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Good day!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I go about charting the sea?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Well, that very much depends. You see, when it comes to charting, there are quite a few different things you'll want to track. What you need to do is going to vary depending on what it is you're tracking.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: That said, the starting point is always going to be your captain's log. I actually helped author the standard log that we give out around the Pandemonium.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: I made sure to include some big hints in there about the things you might find on your sailing adventures.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You say there are a few different things I might want to track. What are they?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Well, you have both big and small points of interest, water currents, sea depths, weather patterns, and then there's also drink crates. They're a curious one that Steve likes people to track.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about charting smaller points of interest?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Well, you'll likely spot some interesting things in the sea and on some of the less explored islands that you find whilst out sailing.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Charting these things is simple. All you need to do is just make a note of them as you find them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about charting larger points of interest?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Ah, for them, you'll want to find a good spot and use a spyglass to take it all in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how do I know where a good spot is?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: That can take a bit of practice, but if you pay close attention, you might find some very subtle, definitely not obvious signs of where the best places are...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about drink crates?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: They're a bit of an interesting one. It's been argued that they're not actually completely essential for charting the sea, but Steve thinks otherwise. Probably best you talk to him if you want to know more.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: They're a bit of an interesting one. On your travels, you may spot some sealed crates floating in the sea.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Once you find one, just pry it open with your crowbar. If you're lucky, there will be a refreshing beverage inside for you to try.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Be careful though, as some of them can be a tinge on the ripe side...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must admit, I'm not totally sure what these crates have to do with charting...

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Yes, that opinion has come up before. However, according to Steve, it's an unwritten rule in this world that alcohol is the key to solving all problems.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: It's also been said that these crates add a bit of much needed danger to the whole charting effort.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about tracking currents?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Absolutely! Understanding the various currents around the seas is essential. As you sail around, you'll likely spot many currents of potential interest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But once I spot them, how do I track them?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Ah, for that, you'll need the right tool. Well, I suppose in this case, it's less of a tool and more of a friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: It will probably make more sense if you head out and discover the art of tracking currents for yourself!

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: We had someone passing through recently who said that Arhein in Catherby was looking to track some currents. I'd suggest you go and speak to him.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: To track them, you'll need to make use of your good friend, the current duck! Specifically, once you've found some currents you want to track, you'll want to let your current duck head out for a swim.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: He'll track the currents for you, so all you need to do is follow him wherever he goes. Once he's done, just pick him up again and let him share what he discovered with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this really the standard way of doing this?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Of course! I simply can't think of any alternatives!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about checking sea depths?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Ah, for that, you'll need to go diving. However, you don't want to rush it. You'll need to make sure you're skilled enough for that sort of thing before you... dive in.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Ah, for that, you'll need to go diving. Doing so will let you take a record of the sea depth, but it's also great for learning more about the local underwater flora and fauna.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: However, I wouldn't reccomend going it alone. Fortunately, a lot of mermaids have been popping up recently, interested in the extra sea traffic.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: I hear they will guide you for a price, and for some inexplicable reason, that price seems to be solving a word-based puzzle for them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A puzzle?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Indeed, so get your thinking cap on... under your diving gear of course. It's very important you don't forget that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about tracking weather patterns?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Well, all good captains need to have a strong understanding of the weather out at sea. However, it requires a fair bit of skill to do it well. I'd suggest you wait until you're more experienced.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Of course! All good captains need to have a strong understanding of the weather out at sea. However, to do it well, you'll probably want to enlist the help of a meteorologist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find one of those?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Well, it's a bit of an odd one, but I've been told there's a troll who has started working as one recently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A troll?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Indeed. It's a bit unusual, but who am I to judge! Anyway, apparently she's aquired a portable weather station, a very fine piece of equipment for measuring weather data.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: If you come across this troll, she'll hopefully allow you to use this weather station for yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how is it used?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Well, what you'll find is that the wind typically blows in large circles. What you need to do is find the middle of this circle, or the eye as we often call it.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: You can use the weather station to work out the wind direction. If you follow it for a bit, you should be able to work out which way the circle is curving, and from that, you should be able to find the eye.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Just remember not to keep following the wind forever, or you'll just keep going around in circles!

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Anyway, once you reach the eye, you can use the weather station to take the readings you need. After that, just make sure to return the weather station to the troll.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about something else.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I get from charting the sea?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Other than the thrill of exploration?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, other than that.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Well, the more you know about the sea, the more you'll be able to take advantage of it. Specifically, learning about the seas will offer the discerning captain a shortcut or two.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: And for the less discerning captain, I have a stock of alcohol I'd be willing to part with for up to date charting information, along with some coins of course.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: My observational skills are not what they used to be after all!

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Finally, in return for charting information, I can offer some training that will help you improve your sailing abilities. Just let me know any time you completely chart an ocean.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you offer me some training?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Sorry, but it doesn't look like you have anything in here that I don't already know.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Yes, it looks like I can...

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Oh, on second thoughts, it doesn't look like you have anything in here that I don't already know.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Yes, it looks like I can...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about these shortcuts?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Absolutely! As you become more familiar with the sea, you will likely start to notice rapid flowing waters where you hadn't before.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Assuming you have a strong enough helm on your boat, sailing over them should give you a noticable speed boost!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about these drinks?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Of course! There's the kraken ink stout, the perildance bitter, the trawler's trust, and of course, the horizon's lure! Which do you want to hear more about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kraken ink stout.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Well, kraken ink stout is the perfect drink if you consider yourself a bit of a magical sailor as it's said to improve both your sailing and magical abilities.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Many claim that it also increases the likelihood of encountering interesting things while out at sea. I'm not sure how a drink does that, but the world works in strange ways.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: While I'd love to sell you some, I'm afraid you need to do some more charting first.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Anyway, I'm pleased to say that you've done enough charting for me to sell you some! Would you like to buy a glass to sample it? Or do you want enough to fill a keg?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Even I can see you don't have space to hold that, and just to be completely clear, I am totally blind!

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: There you go! Enjoy! Now, are you interested in any other drinks?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: : Actually, I'll pass.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Very well. Would you like me to tell you about a different drink?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: There you go! Enjoy! Now, are you interested in any other drinks?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: : Actually, I'll pass.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Very well. Would you like me to tell you about a different drink?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'll pass.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Very well. Would you like me to tell you about a different drink?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Would you like me to tell you about a different drink?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perildance bitter.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Ah, they say perildance bitter is the best drink you'll find for a sailor who likes to get themselves into a fight or two. It will supposedly improve your strength, along with your sailing and ranging abilities.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: But that's not all, for plenty of sailors also claim that a bit of perildance bitter has helped them out of a tight spot by improving their ability to man their boat cannons.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: While I'd love to sell you some, I'm afraid you need to do some more charting first.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Would you like me to tell you about a different drink?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Anyway, I'm pleased to say that you've done enough charting for me to sell you some! Would you like to buy a glass to sample it? Or do you want enough to fill a keg?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'll pass.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Very well. Would you like me to tell you about a different drink?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Trawler's trust.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Well, as the name suggests, trawler's trust is the drink you want if you're planning to head out trawling. It's said to improve both your sailing and fishing abilities.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: I've been told before that drinking it also helps you catch more fish when out deep sea trawling.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: While I'd love to sell you some, I'm afraid you need to do some more charting first.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Would you like me to tell you about a different drink?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Anyway, I'm pleased to say that you've done enough charting for me to sell you some! Would you like to buy a glass to sample it? Or do you want enough to fill a keg?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'll pass.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Very well. Would you like me to tell you about a different drink?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Horizon's lure.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Ah, the old classic. In many ways, there's nothing too fancy about horizon's lure. It's quite simply the perfect drink when all you care about is sailing.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Not only is it said to make you a better sailor, but it apparently also helps you improve your sailing abilities faster.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: While I'd love to sell you some, I'm afraid you need to do some more charting first.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Anyway, I'm pleased to say that you've done enough charting for me to sell you some! Would you like to buy a glass to sample it? Or do you want enough to fill a keg?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Would you like me to tell you about a different drink?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, let's talk about something else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about something else.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about your parrot?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: That's Spot. He's my seeing eye parrot. Say hello, Spot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not very talkative is he?

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Afraid he's mute. He hasn't talked for a good long time, but he still finds ways to express himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Chartin' Charles McAtless
lines.text: Until we meet again!
